data.title: Trump orders critical minerals probe that could lead to tariffs
data.description:
President Donald Trump has directed an investigation to determine if U.S. critical minerals imports "threaten to impair national security," which could lead to tariffs or restrictions on the sector.

Critical minerals - which include lithium, cobalt, nickel, and rare earth elements - are used across the energy, electronics, electric vehicle, and defense industries.

"The U.S. manufacturing and defense industrial bases remain dependent on foreign sources for processed critical mineral products," the order stated, adding that these products face significant global supply chain vulnerabilities and market distortions.

This import dependence "raises the potential for risks to national security, defense readiness, price stability, and economic prosperity and resilience," the order added.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick has been directed to submit a final report to Trump within 180 days of the investigation's commencement. The probe is under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962, following similar ongoing probes on pharmaceuticals and semiconductors.

China currently dominates the critical minerals supply chain, and has been curbing exports as the trade war with the U.S. escalates.

Trump last month signed an executive order to boost domestic mineral production, as the U.S. is 100% import-reliant on at least 15 critical minerals and 70% of U.S. rare earth imports come from China. 

Related stocks: BHP (NYSE:BHP), Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO), Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF), Vale (NYSE:VALE), Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF).

data.title: Thailand mulls increasing LNG imports from US over next 5 years
data.description:
• Thailand plans to import more liquefied natural gas from the United States over the next five years, Finance Minister Pichai Chunhavajira said on Wednesday.
  • Thailand is an LNG importer and wants to be a distributor in the region, and so will have to import more, he added.
  • The remarks come ahead of a planned meeting to discuss tariffs with US officials early next week.
  • There is already an agreement to import 1 million tonnes of LNG worth $500 million next year as part of a 15-year plan starting in 2026, totalling 15 million tonnes, Reuters reported, citing Pichai.
  • The Trump administration has imposed a 36% tariff on Thailand.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Major shareholder seeks Harley-Davidson board shakeup, calls for CEO to step down - report
Theratechnologies to further evaluate potential sale of company
data.description:
• Theratechnologies  (TSX:TH:CA) (NASDAQ:THTX) Tuesday said that it has decided to further evaluate the potential sale of the company through an open and non-exclusive process.
  • In connection with this determination, the Board has authorized the special committee, consisting of independent and disinterested directors to oversee the process and make a recommendation to the full Board.
  • To support this process, in addition to Barclays as financial advisor and Fasken as legal advisor, the Special Committee has also engaged Raymond James as independent financial advisor and Norton Rose Fulbright as independent legal advisor.
  • There is no assurance that discussions with Future Pak or any other interested party will result in a transaction.
  • Theratechnologies  (TSX:TH:CA) (NASDAQ:THTX) added that while these discussions may be ongoing, operations continue in the normal course.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: US lowers estimate of aid provided to Ukraine to $100 billion - report
data.description:
The United States has cut down its cost estimate for the assistance provided to Ukraine since Russia's invasion in 2022 to about $100 billion from $300 billion, Bloomberg reported on Wednesday.

This brings it closer to Ukraine's estimate, which stands at over $90 billion, the report said.

U.S. President Donald Trump is seeking a bilateral minerals deal as part of a peace push to end Russia's war in Ukraine.

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Tuesday that talks with the U.S. regarding a minerals deal were "positive" and that more meetings were expected.

The Trump administration is pushing to control all major future infrastructure and mineral investments in Ukraine, potentially gaining a veto over any role for Kyiv’s other allies and undermining its bid for European Union membership.

The U.S. has been Kyiv's largest supporter since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

data.title: Nutex Health files prospectus for secondary offering
Teanx Therapeutics plans secondary offering
data.description:
• Tenax Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TENX) filed a prospectus for resale by the selling stockholder of up to 378,346 shares of common stock and up to ~3.76M shares of common stock issuable upon the exercise of pre-funded warrants.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale.
  • Source: Form S-3 filing

data.title: China names new top trade negotiator amid tariff tensions with US
data.description:
• China on Wednesday appointed a new top international trade negotiator amid tariff tensions with the U.S.
  • Li Chenggang, a former assistant commerce minister during the first administration of U.S. President Donald Trump, takes over from Wang Shouwen, the human resources and social security ministry said in a statement.
  • The unexpected move comes as China faces 145% taxes on exports to the U.S., while other countries were given a 90-day reprieve for most duties.
  • The change also took place in the middle of President Xi Jinping's tour of Southeast Asia to consolidate economic and trading ties with close neighbours amid the standoff with the U.S.

China ETFs: (KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (ASHR), (YIN), (YANG), (GXC), (FXP), (KBA), (CXSE), (CNYA), (CHAU), (FLCH), (YXI), (FCA), (KALL), (JCHI)

Market-tracking ETFs include:  (NYSEARCA:SPY), (QQQ), (DIA), (VOO), (IVV), (DOG) (SQQQ), (RSP), (UPRO), (SH), and (QLD).

data.description:
• Blackboxstocks (NASDAQ:BLBX) files a prospectus for resale by the selling stockholder of up to 920,000 shares under that certain senior secured convertible debenture in the principal amount of up to $3M.

data.title: Trump tariffs could cost chipmakers more than $1 billion - report
data.description:
• The latest tariffs by President Donald Trump could result in annual losses exceeding $1 billion for American semiconductor manufacturers, according to industry estimates discussed with officials and lawmakers in Washington last week.
  • The three largest U.S. chip equipment makers—Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT), Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX), and KLA (NASDAQ:KLAC)—are each projected to face about $350 million in losses per year owing to such leveis, according to a Reuters report that cites two sources familiar with the matter.  Smaller companies like Onto Innovation (NYSE:ONTO) may also incur additional costs in the tens of millions of dollars, the report added.
  • The main drivers are lost international sales, higher supply chain costs, and increased regulatory compliance expenses. The situation remains fluid as the final details of the tariffs are still being determined.
  • Though the Trump administration has paused some reciprocal tariffs but is considering further duties to encourage domestic manufacturing and has initiated an investigation into chip equipment imports.
  • Top semiconductor ETFs to track: (NASDAQ:SMH), (NASDAQ:SOXX), (NASDAQ:SOXQ), (NYSE:SHOC).
